Home / Services / Vestibular Rehabilitation

Vestibular Rehabilitation

Specialized care for dizziness and balance disorders.

Vertigo and balance problems can make navigating Boise's uneven sidewalks and winter ice particularly challenging. Our vestibular rehabilitation program helps patients regain stability and confidence in movement.

We treat conditions like BPPV (benign paroxysmal positional vertigo), which commonly affects older adults in our community. Using specialized repositioning maneuvers, we can often provide immediate relief from vertigo symptoms.

For complex balance disorders, we develop customized exercise programs that progress from simple to more challenging movements. Our clinic features balance equipment and technology to safely retrain your vestibular system, helping you feel steady on your feet again.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Comprehensive vestibular and balance assessment
  2. 02 Step 2: Specific maneuvers for positional vertigo when indicated
  3. 03 Step 3: Customized habituation and adaptation exercises
  4. 04 Step 4: Functional retraining for home and community safety

Benefits

  • Reduced dizziness and improved balance
  • Increased confidence in daily activities
  • Customized home safety recommendations
  • Strategies to manage symptoms during weather changes

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I know if I need vestibular therapy?

If you experience frequent dizziness, unsteadiness, or nausea with head movements, an evaluation can determine if vestibular rehab would help. Many patients come to us after falls or car accidents.

Is vestibular therapy uncomfortable?

Some exercises may temporarily increase symptoms as your system adapts, but we carefully monitor your response and adjust accordingly. Most patients tolerate treatment well.

Can this help with motion sickness in cars?

Yes, we can train your vestibular system to better tolerate motion, which helps many patients who struggle with Boise's winding foothills roads.

Book an Appointment

Ready to get started with vestibular rehabilitation? Contact us today.

Schedule Now Or call: 555-phy-r92
